title: New Orleans 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: New Orleans 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to New Orleans, United States: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including National WWII Museum and Preservation Hall. Prices from $2035 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering New Orleans, United States — Humid subtropical climate with mild winters (highs around 18°C) and hot, sticky summers (highs 32-33°C with high humidity and frequent afternoon thunderstorms); hurricane season runs June to November. February to May is the sweet spot — Mardi Gras energy in February/March gives way to comfortable spring weather before summer humidity sets in. This itinerary blends National WWII Museum, Preservation Hall, French Quarter streets with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including National WWII Museum, Preservation Hall, French Quarter streets
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in New Orleans
itinerary.description: Land at Louis Armstrong New Orleans International Airport (MSY) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to National WWII Museum — Smithsonian-affiliated museum widely ranked among the best in the US. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: New Orleans: Preservation Hall & Steamboat Natchez Cruise
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Preservation Hall — Legendary intimate traditional jazz venue in the French Quarter. In the afternoon, continue to Steamboat Natchez Cruise — Paddlewheel riverboat cruise on the Mississippi with a live jazz band. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: New Orleans: Audubon Aquarium of the Americas & Audubon Zoo
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Audubon Aquarium of the Americas — Riverfront aquarium with a Gulf of Mexico tank and white alligators. In the afternoon, continue to Audubon Zoo — Uptown zoo with a Louisiana swamp exhibit. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Oak Alley Plantation
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Oak Alley Plantation, a popular side trip from New Orleans. Return to New Orleans in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Oak Alley Plantation

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Bacchanal Wine Garden in Bywater — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: New Orleans: St. Louis Cemetery No. 1 guided tour & Mardi Gras World
itinerary.description: Morning visit to St. Louis Cemetery No. 1 guided tour — Historic above-ground "city of the dead," visit only with a licensed guide. In the afternoon, continue to Mardi Gras World — Working warehouse where parade floats are built, with tours. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: St. Louis Cemetery No. 1 guided tour

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Honey Island Swamp
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Honey Island Swamp, a popular side trip from New Orleans. Return to New Orleans in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Honey Island Swamp

itinerary.title: New Orleans: The Cabildo & Swamp tour (Honey Island Swamp)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to The Cabildo — Louisiana State Museum building on Jackson Square covering Louisiana history. In the afternoon, continue to Swamp tour (Honey Island Swamp) — Boat tour into Louisiana bayou spotting alligators and wildlife. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Backstreet Cultural Museum — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Baton Rouge
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Baton Rouge, a popular side trip from New Orleans. Return to New Orleans in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: New Orleans: Ogden Museum of Southern Art & Hurricane Katrina exhibit at The Presbytère
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Ogden Museum of Southern Art — Dedicated collection of art from the American South. In the afternoon, continue to Hurricane Katrina exhibit at The Presbytère — Louisiana State Museum exhibit on Katrina and hurricanes. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Hurricane Katrina exhibit at The Presbytère

itinerary.title: New Orleans: French Quarter streets & Jackson Square
itinerary.description: Morning visit to French Quarter streets — Historic core with wrought-iron balconies, street musicians and Bourbon Street. In the afternoon, continue to Jackson Square — Central plaza fronting St. Louis Cathedral, ringed by artists and tarot readers. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Longue Vue House and Gardens — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to St. Louis Cathedral exterior or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to MSY for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book Jazz Fest or Mardi Gras accommodation 6-12 months ahead — prices multiply and rooms sell out.
Only tour St. Louis Cemetery No. 1 with an official licensed guide; solo entry has been restricted for years.
Avoid summer (June-August) if humidity and hurricane risk are a concern — spring and fall are far more comfortable.
Ask locals or your hotel for a Frenchmen Street club recommendation rather than Bourbon Street for authentic live jazz.
Carry cash for street musicians, tip buckets and some smaller Creole restaurants that don't take cards.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Lightweight, breathable clothing for heat and humidity
A light rain jacket for sudden summer downpours
Comfortable shoes for uneven French Quarter sidewalks
Costume or bead-friendly outfit if visiting during Mardi Gras
thingsToDo: St. Louis Cemetery No. 1 guided tour
Hurricane Katrina exhibit at The Presbytère
thingsToAvoid: Overpriced, watered-down "Hand Grenade" and hurricane cocktails sold at Bourbon Street tourist bars.
Walking alone late at night outside the well-lit French Quarter and Garden District core.
Street hustlers offering a bet ("I bet I can tell you where you got your shoes") — it's a scripted scam.
Driving into the French Quarter — parking is scarce and expensive; walk or use rideshare instead.
Underestimating hurricane season (June-November) when booking a late-summer trip without travel insurance.
topRestaurants: Willie Mae's Scotch House — Fried Chicken/Soul Food ($)
instagramSpots: French Quarter balconies on Royal Street
Jackson Square with St. Louis Cathedral
adventureActivities: Cycling City Park and the Lafitte Greenway
luxuryExperiences: Private jazz brunch with a chef's table
Luxury suite at The Roosevelt New Orleans
honeymoonExperiences: Candlelit dinner in a French Quarter courtyard restaurant
Evening carriage ride through the Quarter
Live jazz date night at Preservation Hall
Balcony suite stay overlooking Royal Street
budgetExperiences: Beignets and chicory coffee at Cafe du Monde
Free stroll through the French Quarter and Jackson Square
Frenchmen Street live music (many bars have no cover)

festivalCalendar.name: Mardi Gras
festivalCalendar.timing: Late January to early March (Tuesday before Ash Wednesday)

festivalCalendar.name: New Orleans Jazz & Heritage Festival (Jazz Fest)

localEtiquette: Locals say "y'all" and greet strangers with easy small talk — friendliness is genuine, not a hustle.
Tipping 20% is standard at restaurants and for live music performers passing a tip bucket.
Beads and Mardi Gras throws are earned by cheering and interacting with parade floats, not demanded.
Dress is casual almost everywhere except a handful of fine-dining rooms in the French Quarter.
Public alcohol consumption is legal on Bourbon Street and much of the French Quarter, but respect noise rules in residential blocks.
localTransportGuide: The historic St. Charles Avenue streetcar and RTA buses cover most visitor areas cheaply, and the French Quarter and Garden District are best explored on foot. Rideshares are the practical option for the airport and areas outside the streetcar lines, since the city's public transit network is limited beyond the core tourist routes.
bestTimeToVisit: February to May is the sweet spot — Mardi Gras energy in February/March gives way to comfortable spring weather before summer humidity sets in.
weather: Humid subtropical climate with mild winters (highs around 18°C) and hot, sticky summers (highs 32-33°C with high humidity and frequent afternoon thunderstorms); hurricane season runs June to November.
visaInformation: Visa-free ESTA entry for UK, EU, Australian and other Visa Waiver Program passport holders for stays up to 90 days. Indian nationals and most other nationalities require a US B1/B2 visitor visa arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~15.2h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
nearestAirport: Louis Armstrong New Orleans International Airport (MSY)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit New Orleans?
faqs.answer: Visa-free ESTA entry for UK, EU, Australian and other Visa Waiver Program passport holders for stays up to 90 days. Indian nationals and most other nationalities require a US B1/B2 visitor visa arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit New Orleans?
faqs.answer: February to May is the sweet spot — Mardi Gras energy in February/March gives way to comfortable spring weather before summer humidity sets in. Humid subtropical climate with mild winters (highs around 18°C) and hot, sticky summers (highs 32-33°C with high humidity and frequent afternoon thunderstorms); hurricane season runs June to November.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is New Orleans safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: New Orleans is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to New Orleans?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Nadia Dubois
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: New Orleans is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Preservation Hall. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Olivia Fernandez
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Our trip to New Orleans was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to City Park was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Omar Santos
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond The Cabildo and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Arjun Fernandez
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Booked this as a group trip and it was well paced. City Park in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.
